_comment: Plugin MCP wiring. The write-capable ad platforms (Google Ads + Meta Ads) use Pipeboard's hosted MCP servers, which speak Claude's native remote-MCP OAuth flow: the marketer clicks Connect, signs in with their own Google/Meta account, and Pipeboard brokers the platform OAuth WITHOUT ever seeing the credentials and WITHOUT any Google Ads developer-token application. This is true per-user auth, which is what the spend-gate relies on. Google Drive is NOT wired here - it lives in Claude desktop's native Connectors catalog (Customize -> Connectors -> Google Drive). NOTE on why this is not Composio: Claude desktop's Connect button only performs an OAuth handshake and has no field for an x-api-key / Bearer header (anthropics/claude-ai-mcp #112). Composio's hosted MCP endpoints authenticate with an x-api-key header, so they cannot be authorized from the Connect button - that mismatch caused the dynamic-client-registration failures. GA4 / Search Console / Tag Manager / Merchant Center do not yet have a verified OAuth MCP wired (see _pending_connectors below and CONNECTORS.md); GSC data is already reachable through the Ahrefs MCP.

google-ads._comment: Pipeboard Google Ads MCP. WRITE-CAPABLE: reads + create/update/pause campaigns, budgets, bids, negatives, ads. Per-user OAuth on first Connect (no developer token; Pipeboard provisions under its own GCP project and never stores the user's Google credentials). Requires a free Pipeboard account at pipeboard.co on first connect. Tier 1/2 spend-gate applies via the /ad-actions skill. Docs: https://pipeboard.co/guides/google-ads-mcp

meta-ads._comment: Pipeboard Meta Ads MCP. WRITE-CAPABLE: create/update/pause campaigns, budgets, ads/creatives across Facebook + Instagram. Per-user OAuth on first Connect (no Meta developer app needed). Requires the same free Pipeboard account. Docs: https://pipeboard.co/guides/meta-ads-mcp

ahrefs._comment: SEO keyword research, backlinks, site audits, Brand Radar (AI mentions/GEO) AND Google Search Console data (gsc-* tools) - so GSC organic clicks/impressions/positions are reachable here without a separate GSC connector. Vendor-native MCP. Org API key.

similarweb._comment: Competitive traffic, market benchmarking. Vendor-native MCP. Org API key.

notion._comment: Optional. Briefs, playbooks in Notion. Vendor-native MCP.

supabase._comment: Already in use. Portfolio-site product/usage data. Vendor-native MCP.

vercel._comment: Already in use. Inspect deployments / scheduled data-pull jobs. Vendor-native MCP.

slack._comment: Optional. Share finished reports as messages. Vendor-native MCP.

google-search-console._comment: Self-hosted GSC (gsc-mcp/, FastMCP Google OAuth-proxy). Per-user: marketers click Connect and sign in with Google, nothing to paste. Read-only (webmasters.readonly). Persistent (Fly volume) -> sign in once.

_pending_connectors._comment: NOT wired yet - do NOT ship guessed URLs. Each needs a verified OAuth MCP endpoint (one a marketer can authorize from Claude desktop's Connect button) chosen and tested before team rollout. Candidates are listed for evaluation; once a URL is confirmed working in Claude desktop, move the entry up into mcpServers. See CONNECTORS.md section 'Pending connectors' for the decision log.

candidates: self-hosted google-analytics-mcp with Advanced-settings OAuth client ID

google-tag-manager.purpose: WRITE-CAPABLE: read containers/tags/triggers, create/edit, publish versions. Conversion-tracking changes are Tier 1 in the spend-gate.
google-tag-manager.note: No verified Connect-button OAuth MCP confirmed yet. Until one is wired, Tier-1 GTM tracking edits via /ad-actions are unavailable. Block team rollout of GTM writes until verified.

google-merchant-center.purpose: WRITE-CAPABLE: feed health, product attributes, supplemental feeds, promotions. Only relevant for brands running Shopping/PMax with a product feed.
google-merchant-center.note: Candidate fallback: the official Google Content API via a self-hosted MCP with Advanced-settings OAuth client ID.
